title: Invalid streaming operator packing ordering
body:
> Can you attach an example that shows the issue?

Example:

```verilog
module data_packing;

    byte    data [];
    longint word;

    initial begin

        data = new[8]('{8'hfa, 8'hde, 8'hca, 8'hfe,
                        8'hde, 8'had, 8'hbe, 8'hef});

        word = {>>{data}};
        $display("word: %h", word);
        word = {<<8{data}};
        $display("word: %h", word);

    end

endmodule
```

Expected outptut:

```
word: fadecafedeadbeef
word: efbeaddefecadefa
```

According to 1800-2023 LRM, section 11.4.14.2: _>> causes blocks of data to be streamed in left-to-right order, while << causes blocks of data to be streamed in right-to-left order._

Real output:

```
word: efbeaddefecadefa
word: fadecafedeadbeef
```

> What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```bash
verilator data_packing.sv --binary
./obj_dir/Vdata_packing
```

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

`Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-69-g780163b91`

> What OS and distribution are you using?

CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009.
